title: ECHOES Consultation completed
content:
The publication of the ECHOES Report on identification of communities and their needs concludes the ECHOES Consultation. A booklet presenting the main results of the Consultation and the community-building strategy will be available soon.

The consultation used a mixed-method and iterative approach combining large-scale quantitative data collection through an international survey, qualitative analysis of open ended responses, and targeted validation through workshops, focus groups and  complementary consultation activities. This approach enabled both a broad mapping of the cultural heritage landscape and an in-depth understanding of community practices, needs and expectations for the Cultural Heritage Cloud.

In addition, a separate annex, Cluster profiles analysis, is also available which presents the full set of cluster profiles developed as part of the analysis. Each profile represents a distinct community configuration identified through the systematic analysis of survey responses, combining socio-professional characteristics, data practices and technological engagement.

creator: Sheena_B
sourceDescription: European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science

title: ECHOES for Portuguese-speaking Communities
content:
ECHOES and ICOM Portugal hosted a highly successful webinar “ECHOES for Portuguese-speaking Communities” on Tuesday 16th June and would like to thank all participants for their presence, interest and contributions.

The high level of participation and the quality of the discussions demonstrated the growing interest of Portuguese-speaking heritage communities in following and contributing to the development of the future European Cultural Heritage Cloud. The dialogue established during the session helped identify expectations, needs and opportunities for collaboration, all of which are essential to ensure an active and representative engagement of these communities in this process.

This meeting marked an important first step in strengthening connections between professionals, institutions and heritage networks across different Portuguese-speaking countries and regions, fostering the exchange of experiences and the development of new partnerships around the digital transformation of the sector.

To learn more and further explore opportunities for engagement, download the presentations given at the webinar.

title: Digitizing Historical Libraries in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
content:
ECHOES was mentioned extensively throughout the talk, particularly:

  • the ECHOES Consultation, as one of the most recent attempts at mapping digital cultural heritage practices and needs across Europe,
  • the diversity and fragmentation of the cultural heritage ecosystem revealed through the consultation findings,
  • the Collaborative Research Scenarios as examples of practice-oriented, cross-domain research workflows and infrastructure requirements,
  • the implications of these findings for the future development of digital cultural heritage ecosystems and cloud-based infrastructures.

    • More information: Conference website.

the ECHOES Consultation, as one of the most recent attempts at mapping digital cultural heritage practices and needs across Europe,

title: ECHOES User Needs Workshop at the DARIAH Annual Event
content:
ECHOES hosted a workshop at the DARIAH Annual Event in Rome on the 26th of May 2026 to examine how user needs relating to the Cultural Heritage Cloud are identified, interpreted, and translated into infrastructure development. Rather than seeking a single methodology, participants explored commonalities, differences, and ways to improve alignment between user-needs research and technical implementation.

After the participants identified a number of key findings, these were prioritised and the next steps agreed upon.

The workshop concluded that ECHOES must now move beyond understanding user needs and focus on systematically converting those needs into validated services, workflows, and infrastructure improvements through continuous engagement with user communities.

title: Online Focus Group for Networks and Umbrella Organisations
content:
An online focus group hosting representatives of ECHOES networks and umbrella organisation was held on the 8th April 11:00-13:00 CET.

It gathered participants from the following networks/umbrella organisations:

  • APEF – Archives Portal Foundation (archiving and digital curation),
  • ARIADNE (archaeology RI),
  • ECCO European C (conservators and restorers)
  • ICOM International -AVICOM, (International Committee for Audiovisual, New Technologies and Social Media)
  • ICOM Portugal (museums),
  • Michael Culture Association (digital cultural heritage),
  • NEMO (Working Group on Digital Transformation (museums)).

    • All participants had been invited due to their involvement in the digital transformation actions and strategies within their organisation. They were informed in advance of the questions to be discussed during the session.

    After a short presentation on the Cultural Heritage Cloud and the Consultation survey by Rémi Petitcol and Charlotte Gallini, and an introduction to the Cloud’s architecture by Joanna Kowalska, the floor was opened to discussions and exchanges and a structured dialogue was conducted around three main questions:

title: ECHOES Baltics & Nordics Event at the NEMO Annual Conference
content:
The ECHOES event ‘The Cultural Heritage Cloud regional meeting – Connecting to the Baltics and Nordics‘ on developing a European digital cloud for cultural heritage will be held on the 14th October, the day after the NEMO European Museum Conference being held in Vilnius, Lithuania from Sunday 11th October- Tuesday 13th October 2026.

Following the official conference programme, one of NEMO’s current projects, ECHOES, is offering an additional session on its work developing a European digital cloud for cultural heritage.

How can museums and cultural heritage institutions across Europe collaborate more effectively in the digital age?

This meeting invites museum professionals, researchers, and policymakers to explore new opportunities for connection, exchange, and collaboration. Taking advantage of the conference location, the meeting primarily welcomes professionals from the Baltic and Nordic countries, thereby taking on a regional, as well as an international, character.

The session introduces the ECHOES project within the European cultural heritage and research policy landscape, opening a dialogue with regional stakeholders and practitioners.

title: ECHOES at the Forefront at the Digital Heritage Summit 2026
content:
ECHOES along with six Sister Projects made a strong impact at the Digital Heritage Summit event held in Limassol, Cyprus at the end of May with involvement in several of the round tables and discussions.

The four-day Digital Heritage Summit took place from 26th-29th May 2026 during the Cypriot Presidency of the Council of the EU and under the auspices of the Deputy Minister of Culture. It featured addresses from EU representatives, UNESCO, and international institutional partners and networks. The programme of high-level dialogue, strategic policy discussions, hands-on experimentation, and collaborative innovation highlighted Europe’s leading position in the digitisation of Cultural Heritage.

title: ECHOES Webinar for Portuguese-Speaking Cultural Heritage Institutions
content:
The ECHOES – European Cloud for Heritage OpEn Science project is pleased to invite you to participate in a webinar aimed at cultural heritage professionals and museum institutions from Portuguese-speaking countries and regions. The event will take place on 16th June 2026, from 17:00 to 19:00 (WEST) via Zoom.

ECHOES is a European Union-funded project that is developing the future European Collaborative Cloud for Cultural Heritage, a shared digital infrastructure designed to support the access, sharing, preservation and reuse of cultural heritage data and digital resources, while fostering research, innovation and international cooperation across the sector.

The webinar is organised by ICOM Portugal, a member of the ECHOES Consortium, in partnership with FSP and E.C.C.O., and seeks to promote dialogue and cooperation among cultural heritage professionals and institutions, contributing to the development of this European infrastructure.

The main objectives of the webinar are:

  • To connect cultural heritage institutions across Portuguese-speaking regions within the framework of the ECHOES project;
  • To establish links with non-European partners around Digital Commons for Cultural Heritage;

title: Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP)
content:
The Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP), which will take place from 9–11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum (Germany) is open until the 15th June. 

The Culture Community Plenary (CCP), taking place from 9 to 11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum, is NFDI4Culture’s largest annual event. It serves as a central forum for exchange between the consortium and the community, as well as among community members themselves. The Plenary offers opportunities for networking, reporting on achievements, and shaping the future of the initiative. Projects and initiatives in the fields of tangible and intangible cultural heritage are invited to present their work to the 4Culture community. For CCP6, we are offering a variety of formats to strengthen collaborative research and foster broad knowledge exchange.

Abstracts are welcomed (1–2 pages) on the following topics:

  • Research data projects in the culture disciplines at any stage
    • We especially encourage interdisciplinary initiatives and concrete use cases for data reuse!
  • We especially encourage interdisciplinary initiatives and concrete use cases for data reuse!
snippet: The Call for Abstracts for the 6th Culture Community Plenary (CCP), which will take place from 9–11 November 2026 at the Deutsches Bergbau-Museum Bochum (Germany) is open until the 15th June.
